info.title: WIP Registry Service
info.description:
## World In a Pie - Federated Identity Registry

The Registry service provides centralized identity management for the WIP ecosystem.

### Key Features

- **Namespaces**: Manage entity namespaces with configurable ID algorithms
- **Composite Key Registration**: Register any combination of fields as an identity
- **Synonym Support**: Multiple keys can resolve to the same entity
- **ID-as-Synonym (Merge)**: Resolve duplicate registrations
- **Cross-Namespace Search**: Find entities across all namespaces
- **ID Provisioning**: Registry generates IDs per namespace config
- **ID Reservation**: Clients provide IDs, registry validates format
- **Pluggable ID Generation**: UUID4, UUID7, NanoID, Prefixed, or Pattern-based

### Authentication

All endpoints require API key authentication via the `X-API-Key` header.

Admin operations (namespace management) require elevated privileges.

delete.description:
Delete a namespace and all its data.

Requires deletion_mode='full' on the namespace. Creates a persistent
journal for crash-safe execution across MongoDB, MinIO, and PostgreSQL.

- dry_run=true: Returns impact report (entity counts, inbound references)
- force=true: Proceeds even if other namespaces reference this one

parameters.name: dry_run

schema.description: Return impact report without making changes
schema.title: Dry Run

parameters.description: Return impact report without making changes

patch.description:
Update a namespace's deletion_mode.

Changing retain -> full requires confirm_enable_deletion=true.
The 'wip' namespace is always retain and cannot be changed.

get.description: Get a specific namespace by its prefix.

put.description:
Upsert a namespace's configuration.

If the namespace exists, update the fields supplied in the body
(only fields explicitly provided are touched). If the namespace
does not exist, create it with the supplied fields and defaults
for anything omitted (`isolation_mode='open'`, `deletion_mode='retain'`,
`description=''`, `allowed_external_refs=[]`, `id_config=None`).

This makes app bootstrap scripts idempotent — a single self-healing
call replaces the `GET → 404 → POST` dance.

get.description: Get the current journal state for an in-progress or completed deletion.

post.description:
Resume an incomplete deletion from where it left off.

Used when a backend was unavailable during the initial attempt.

get.description: List namespaces the caller can access.

schema.title: Response List Namespaces Api Registry Namespaces Get

post.description:
Create a new namespace with optional ID algorithm configuration.

If id_config is not provided, defaults to UUID7 for all entity types.

get.description: Get entity counts for each entity type in the namespace.
Get ID algorithm configuration for a namespace. Services cache this at startup.

schema.title: Response Get Namespace Id Config Api Registry Namespaces  Prefix  Id Config Get

post.description: Archive a namespace. No new entries can be created.
Restore an archived namespace.
Initialize the default 'wip' namespace with UUID7 for all entity types.
Export a namespace to a downloadable archive.

get.description: Download an exported namespace archive.

post.description: Import a namespace from an exported archive.

schema.title: Response List Grants Api Registry Namespaces  Prefix  Grants Get

get.description: Browse registry entries with pagination and optional filters.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search across entry IDs and composite key values
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search across entry IDs and composite key values

put.description: Update one or more registry entries.

delete.summary: Delete entries (bulk, soft or hard delete)
delete.description:
Deactivate or hard-delete one or more registry entries.

Hard-delete (hard_delete=True) permanently removes the entry from MongoDB.
Requires the entry's namespace to have deletion_mode='full'.

get.summary: Unified search across entry IDs, composite keys, and synonyms
get.description:
Unified search across entry IDs, additional IDs, and all composite key values
(primary + synonyms). Returns rich results with match context and resolution paths.

get.summary: Get a single registry entry by ID
get.description: Get full details for a single registry entry by its entry_id.

post.description:
Register one or more composite keys. This is sugar for reserve + immediate activate.

For each key:
- If the key already exists, returns the existing registry ID
- If new, generates an ID (or uses client-provided one) and creates an active entry

Provision (generate + reserve) IDs per namespace config.

Registry generates IDs according to the namespace's configured algorithm.
Returns reserved entries that must be activated after entity creation.

Validate and store client-provided IDs as reserved.

Validates each ID against the namespace's configured format.
Activate reserved entries, making them resolvable.
Look up registry entries by their IDs. Only active entries are resolvable.
Look up registry entries by their composite keys.

post.summary: Resolve synonym composite keys to entry IDs (bulk)
post.description:
Resolve synonyms or verify canonical IDs.

Each item may provide ``entry_id`` (canonical ID verification),
``composite_key`` (synonym resolution), or both.  When both are
given, ``entry_id`` is tried first.

Returns entry_id for each found item, "not_found" otherwise.
Add one or more synonyms to existing registry entries.
Remove one or more synonyms from registry entries.
Merge two entries, making the deprecated one a synonym of the preferred.
Search for registry entries by field values in composite keys.
Search for a term across any field in any composite key.
Search for entries across ALL namespaces.

get.description: List all namespaces the caller has access to, with permission levels.

schema.title: Response My Namespaces Api Registry My Namespaces Get

get.summary: Get my permission on a specific namespace
get.description:
Get the caller's permission level on a specific namespace.

Returns 404 if the namespace doesn't exist or the caller has no access
(to avoid leaking namespace names).

get.summary: Check permission for a given identity (internal, service-to-service)
get.description:
Internal endpoint for other WIP services to check permissions.

Services call this to resolve a user's permission on a namespace
without that user being the direct caller.

Groups are read from the X-User-Groups header (preferred) or the
groups query parameter (legacy, deprecated).

Only callable by privileged API keys (wip-admins or wip-services group).

get.summary: Get accessible namespaces for a given identity (internal, service-to-service)
get.description:
Internal endpoint for services to get a user's accessible namespaces.

Returns the list of namespace prefixes the user can access, plus
whether they are a superadmin (meaning: access to everything).

Groups are read from X-User-Groups header (preferred) or groups query param.

Only callable by privileged API keys (wip-admins or wip-services group).
List all API keys (config + runtime). No hashes returned.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Api Registry Api Keys Get

post.description: Create a new runtime API key. The plaintext is returned once and never stored.

get.summary: Sync endpoint for service key polling
get.description:
Return enabled runtime keys with hashes for service polling.

Only returns runtime keys — config keys are already loaded by each service.
Requires wip-services or wip-admins group (enforced by require_api_key +
the caller must be a service key).

schema.title: Response Sync Api Keys Api Registry Api Keys Sync Get

get.summary: Get a single API key by name
get.description: Get metadata for a single API key (config or runtime).

patch.description: Update metadata on a runtime API key. Config-file keys cannot be modified.

delete.description: Hard-delete a runtime API key. Config-file keys cannot be deleted.

schema.title: Response Delete Api Key Api Registry Api Keys  Name  Delete

get.description: Root endpoint with service information.

Health check endpoint.

Verifies MongoDB connectivity and returns service status.

Health check endpoint.

Verifies MongoDB connectivity and returns service status.

Readiness check endpoint.

Returns 200 when the service is ready to accept traffic.

Request model for resolving a synonym or canonical ID to an entry ID.
